item.title: Keep stealing the days away from discomfort
item.dc:creator: thisisyourbrainspeaking

item.title: Heard you weirdos like old timey historical photos.
item.description:
Found all these in an ancient family photo album. Not sure who most of the people are but I understand I’m related to most of them. This photo strikes me because of the date. 3 days before the stock market crash. Look at how carefree they were.
A relative wearing his uniform from WW1.
Apparently I had a great aunt who died in childbirth. The baby boy was going to be named Aloysius. Neither of them survived. Her remaining children often visited the grave of their mother.
The writing on this photo is pretty self explanatory.
It’s fun to think about people having pets more than 100 years ago. And guinea pigs no less!
This photo is from the early 1920s. People were looking pretty fly for a hike!
Just guys being dudes.
This one struck me because that’s actually my grandfather on the left. I’ll be hiking at Mount Baldy this week for the first time. It’s been almost 100 years since that photo. As far as I know no member of my family has been there since.
Gotta love the beachwear.
I wish I had more context for this photo. The film “A League of Their Own” comes to mind. “Marla Hooch...what a hitter!!”
Just a sled ride in the Southern California mountains.
Snow day!
This photo is from 1907 or 1908.
Feed a deer? Why not?
I like knowing that even more than 100 years ago people were still total goofballs.
I’d love to know what they ate at that picnic.
Cold beer is timeless.
Every always seemed dressed up.
I bet they were up to no good.
A beer and a smoke. Unwinding after a long day.
Classy car.
Santa Monica has changed A LOT since this photo was taken.
Dog tax!!!
item.dc:creator: idonotsow

item.title: Be like jay and silent Bob
item.dc:creator: specialagentclunk

item.title: This is what the movie "Idiocracy" was trying to warn us about ....
item.dc:creator: blank666man

item.title: Adopted & returned 4 times in 2 weeks
item.description:
A friend sent me this post of Tic Tac on Saturday from an area rescue. She knew I was looking for a senior pup. Tic Tac is an old lady who deserves so much more than humans have given her.
Obviously I immediately went to get her. I'm not a monster. Here she is on her freedom ride. Her smile broke and healed my heart all at once.
In her new home adjusting (with Waffle House leftovers lol). The crate will go away as soon as she's fully comfortable here.
On a walk with her new brother.
The dumbest part of this was the reason she kept getting returned. Literally "She's a velcro dog, she just wants to follow you around and it's annoying." Yes, how awful to have a dog who wants to be with you. People are trash. We are her forever home. She's 11 years old and may not have long left, but whatever she has left will be here, peaceful, and full of love. She's a good girl.
item.dc:creator: Bunicus

item.title: How can you consider your state progressive when housing costs are unaffordable and you refuse to build enough new homes to lower prices?
item.description:
https://nimby.report/preston

item.title: Biden has been INCREDIBLY successful. Don't let Republicans/Russians control the narrative.
item.description: Russian propaganda mills are attempting to make "He's not perfect" seem like "He's just as bad as Trump" in order to drive down engagement, which works directly to help the Republican agenda. Don't fall for it!
item.dc:creator: Nobodyn0se
